entries.title: St. Jerome, Commentary on Daniel, CHAPTER THIRTEEN on daniel 13:22
entries.excerpt: Verse 22. "'For if I do this, it is death to me; but if I do not..." She speaks of sin as death. For just as in the case of one who commits adultery, the adultery means death, so also every sin which results in death is to be equated with death. And we believe we die as often as we sin unto death. And therefore on the other hand we rise again and are made alive just as often as we perform deeds which are worthy of life.

entries.title: STROMATEIS 10 (VIA ON DANIEL 13:22) on daniel 13:22
entries.excerpt: He calls sin death. In the same way, therefore, that for the one who commits adultery, adultery is death, so every sin that leads to death must be called death. And we are convinced that we die every time we sin mortally, whereas we rise again and recover life every time we do works worthy of life.
